title: euphorbia verrucosa subsp. mariolensis
intro: Euphorbia verrucosa subsp. mariolensis, also known as the spotted spurge, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Euphorbia verrucosa subsp. mariolensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are used in various medicinal preparations, including creams and ointments.
